facade_colour: dark brown/red-brown brick with white/cream painted wood trim

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as an early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects architectural styles of the period. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a row, the properties retain building features including their shared mansard roofline, projecting dormer windows, covered porch that extends across all four properties, and first storey bay windows. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a flat, consistent with its house-form, row typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double hung, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include original windows, decorative brick, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a row, the properties retain building features including their shared mansard roofline, projecting dormer windows, covered porch that extends across all four properties, and first storey bay windows. Each property has value as an early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects architectural styles of the period.

facade_description: Row two-storey house-form in dark brown/red-brown brick with white/cream painted wood trim with a flat roof. Features include a double-height three-sided projecting bay and a simple cornice. Some modern elements; overall row form maintained. Constructed 1904-1913.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a row, the properties retain building features including their shared mansard roofline, projecting dormer windows, covered porch that extends across all four properties, and first storey bay windows. Each property has value as an early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects architectural styles of the period.
